Main

# --- Loading Data ---
library(readxl)
SuraData <- read_excel("SuraData/SuraUpdatedData-6.xlsx")
# --- Creating new data sets for each CXT rubric categories without null values ---
CXT1_clean <- SuraData[!is.na(SuraData[[7]]), ]
CXT2a_clean <- SuraData[!is.na(SuraData[[9]]), ]
CXT2b_clean <- SuraData[!is.na(SuraData[[11]]), ]
CXT3_clean <- SuraData[!is.na(SuraData[[13]]), ]
CXT4a_clean <- SuraData[!is.na(SuraData[[15]]), ]
CXT4b_clean <- SuraData[!is.na(SuraData[[17]]), ]

# --- Converting AI variable from numeric to categorical  ---
CXT1_clean$AI  <- as.factor(CXT1_clean$AI)
CXT2a_clean$AI <- as.factor(CXT2a_clean$AI)
CXT2b_clean$AI <- as.factor(CXT2b_clean$AI)
CXT3_clean$AI  <- as.factor(CXT3_clean$AI)
CXT4a_clean$AI <- as.factor(CXT4a_clean$AI)
CXT4b_clean$AI <- as.factor(CXT4b_clean$AI)

# --- Naming each categories for AI variable ---
levels(CXT1_clean$AI)  <- c("Students cannot use AI", "Not Specified", "Students can use AI")
levels(CXT2a_clean$AI) <- c("Students cannot use AI", "Not Specified", "Students can use AI")
levels(CXT2b_clean$AI) <- c("Students cannot use AI", "Not Specified", "Students can use AI")
levels(CXT3_clean$AI)  <- c("Students cannot use AI", "Not Specified", "Students can use AI")
levels(CXT4a_clean$AI) <- c("Students cannot use AI", "Not Specified", "Students can use AI")
levels(CXT4b_clean$AI) <- c("Students cannot use AI", "Not Specified", "Students can use AI")

# --- Fit linear models of relationships between each CXT outcomes and AI usage levels---
model1  <- lm(CXT1  ~ AI, data = CXT1_clean)
model2a <- lm(CXT2a ~ AI, data = CXT2a_clean)
model2b <- lm(CXT2b ~ AI, data = CXT2b_clean)
model3  <- lm(CXT3  ~ AI, data = CXT3_clean)
model4a <- lm(CXT4a ~ AI, data = CXT4a_clean)
model4b <- lm(CXT4b ~ AI, data = CXT4b_clean)

# --- Summaries of the linear models created above to obtain p-values that determines rather the model is statistically significant or not---
summary(model1)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = CXT1 ~ AI, data = CXT1_clean)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -1.6818 -0.6539 -0.1538  0.3462  1.3461 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                       Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)            2.68182    0.18822  14.248  < 2e-16 ***
## AINot Specified       -0.02797    0.20353  -0.137 0.891028    
## AIStudents can use AI -0.99432    0.29007  -3.428 0.000959 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.6243 on 81 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1777, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1574 
## F-statistic: 8.754 on 2 and 81 DF,  p-value: 0.0003614
summary(model2a)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = CXT2a ~ AI, data = CXT2a_clean)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -1.13636 -0.52727 -0.02727  0.47273  1.36364 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                       Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)             2.6364     0.1833  14.379   <2e-16 ***
## AINot Specified        -0.1091     0.2008  -0.543    0.589    
## AIStudents can use AI  -0.1364     0.2826  -0.483    0.631    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.6081 on 71 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.004694,   Adjusted R-squared:  -0.02334 
## F-statistic: 0.1674 on 2 and 71 DF,  p-value: 0.8462
summary(model2b)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = CXT2b ~ AI, data = CXT2b_clean)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -1.6818 -0.2329  0.3182  0.3182  1.3182 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                         Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)            2.682e+00  1.633e-01  16.426   <2e-16 ***
## AINot Specified        8.863e-17  1.788e-01   0.000   1.0000    
## AIStudents can use AI -4.318e-01  2.516e-01  -1.716   0.0905 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.5415 on 71 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.06007,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.03359 
## F-statistic: 2.269 on 2 and 71 DF,  p-value: 0.1109
summary(model3)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = CXT3 ~ AI, data = CXT3_clean)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -1.6364 -0.6364 -0.1250  0.8636  1.8636 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                       Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)             2.2727     0.2994   7.592 4.46e-11 ***
## AINot Specified        -0.6364     0.3233  -1.968   0.0524 .  
## AIStudents can use AI  -0.1477     0.4613  -0.320   0.7496    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.9928 on 82 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.05787,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.03489 
## F-statistic: 2.518 on 2 and 82 DF,  p-value: 0.08682
summary(model4a)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = CXT4a ~ AI, data = CXT4a_clean)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -1.7615 -0.2615 -0.2615  0.2385  1.2385 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                       Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)             1.8636     0.1691  11.022   <2e-16 ***
## AINot Specified         0.3979     0.1828   2.176   0.0324 *  
## AIStudents can use AI  -0.4886     0.2606  -1.875   0.0644 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.5608 on 81 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.2024, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1827 
## F-statistic: 10.28 on 2 and 81 DF,  p-value: 0.0001054
summary(model4b)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = CXT4b ~ AI, data = CXT4b_clean)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -1.92045 -0.42045  0.07955  0.57955  1.12500 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                 Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)       1.9205     0.1259  15.259  < 2e-16 ***
## AINot Specified  -1.0455     0.3209  -3.258  0.00202 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.8348 on 50 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1751, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1586 
## F-statistic: 10.62 on 1 and 50 DF,  p-value: 0.002019
# --- Box plot visualizations for each CXT learning outcomes ---
boxplot(CXT1 ~ AI, data = CXT1_clean,
        names = c("Students cannot use AI", "Not Specified", "Students can use AI"),
        main = "Points by AI Use for CXT1",
        xlab = "AI Category",
        ylab = "Points",
        col = c("lightblue", "lightgreen", "lightyellow"))

boxplot(CXT2a ~ AI, data = CXT2a_clean,
        names = c("Students cannot use AI", "Not Specified", "Students can use AI"),
        main = "Points by AI Use for CXT2a",
        xlab = "AI Category",
        ylab = "Points",
        col = c("lightblue", "lightgreen", "lightyellow"))

boxplot(CXT2b ~ AI, data = CXT2b_clean,
        names = c("Students cannot use AI", "Not Specified", "Students can use AI"),
        main = "Points by AI Use for CXT2b",
        xlab = "AI Category",
        ylab = "Points",
        col = c("lightblue", "lightgreen", "lightyellow"))

boxplot(CXT3 ~ AI, data = CXT3_clean,
        names = c("Students cannot use AI", "Not Specified", "Students can use AI"),
        main = "Points by AI Use for CXT3",
        xlab = "AI Category",
        ylab = "Points",
        col = c("lightblue", "lightgreen", "lightyellow"))

boxplot(CXT4a ~ AI, data = CXT4a_clean,
        names = c("Students cannot use AI", "Not Specified", "Students can use AI"),
        main = "Points by AI Use for CXT4a",
        xlab = "AI Category",
        ylab = "Points",
        col = c("lightblue", "lightgreen", "lightyellow"))

boxplot(CXT4b ~ AI, data = CXT4b_clean,
        names = c("Students cannot use AI", "Not Specified", "Students can use AI"),
        main = "Points by AI Use for CXT4b",
        xlab = "AI Category",
        ylab = "Points",
        col = c("lightblue", "lightgreen", "lightyellow"))
